Manx: CARVAL MYCHIONE ÇHEET CREEST.
English: A CAROL CONCERNING CHRIST’S COMING.
Notes: [Above reading suggested by Mr. P. W. Caine.]

Manx: Yn oyr t’er hayrn shin cooidjagh jiu
English: The cause that has drawn us together to-day

Manx: Cha nee myr gien, craid, spoyrt ny cloie,
English: Is not as a feast, a mock, a sport or play,

Manx: Agh dy smaghtaghey yn olk eajee
English: But to correct the hateful evil

Manx: Ta reill dy follit ayns nyn gree
English: That rules concealed in our hearts

Manx: Nagh mooar yn treih
English: Is not the sadness great

Manx: Dy vel yn sleih
English: That people will

Manx: Cha mennick craid ayns feanish Yee!
English: So often scoff in the presence of God!

Manx: Son paart v’er jeet ayns shoh dy chraid,
English: For some have come here to scoff,

Manx: As paart trooid cliaghtey cloie as spoyrt.
English: And some through custom play and sport.

Manx: Fer tayrn fer elley gys yn olk.
English: One man draws another to the evil.

Manx: Myr shoh ta earroo mooar nyn vud,
English: Thus there is a great number amongst us

Manx: Nagh vel cur geill
English: [Who] do not give heed

Manx: Dy lhaih ’sy cheeill.
English: To the reading in the church

Manx: Ta’n reamys shoh currit da’n eill.
English: This freedom is given to the flesh.

Manx: Son corrym rish yn laa mie t’ayn
English: For like as on the present holy [lit: good] day (i.e. on another Christmas Eve.)

Manx: Paart foddyms genmys va ’syn cheeill
English: Some I could name [who] were in the church

Manx: Va jannoo olk as pluckey folt,
English: Were doing ill and plucking hair (rowdy horse-play).

Manx: Agh yn jouyl hene va moir yn olk
English: But the devil himself, who was the author [lit: mother] of the evil

Manx: Cur ayns nyn gree
English: Put [it] into their heart

Manx: Feoh y choyrt da Jee
English: To abhor God

Manx: Ayns craid tranlaasagh shin va guee.
English: In scorn troubling us [who] were praying.

Manx: Bwooishins ny goan shoh dy ghoaill greme
English: I would wish these words to take hold

Manx: As lhiantyn ayns cree dagh unnane.
English: And cleave to the heart of each one [of you].

Manx: Cha vel yn ooashley curt da’n Jouyl
English: The worship given to the devil

Manx: Yn shirveish lhisagh ve ec y Çhiarn.
English: Is not the service that should be [given] to the Lord.

Manx: O lhiassee-jee
English: O amend ye

Manx: Cre erbee
English: Whatever

Manx: Yn fardail t’er n’yientyn ayns nyn gree.
English: Vanity that is conceived in your hearts.

Manx: Dy phlooghey dagh smooinaghtyn er olk
English: To smother each evil thought

Manx: As dy chur dagh smooinaghtyn mie er-ash
English: And to cherish [lit: revive] each good thought

Manx: As obbyr chooie yn laa t’ayn jean jiu,
English: And to do the due service of the present day

Manx: As veih nyn obbyr dy ghoaill aash.
English: And to take rest from our [usual] work.

Manx: Dagh aeg as shenn
English: Each [one of us] young and old

Manx: Lesh creeaghyn glen
English: With pure hearts

Manx: Veagh gennal jeant un cheayrt ayns blein.
English: Would be made glad once in a year.

Manx: Son corrym rish yn moghrey shoh
English: For like as at this morning

Manx: Rug Creest veih oghrish yn Ayr.
English: Christ was born [coming] from the Father’s bosom

Manx: Dy chosney shin veih pooar yn noid
English: To win us from the power of the enemy

Manx: As dy gheddyn dooin yn eiraght chair
English: And to obtain for us the right heritage

Manx: Shin caillit va
English: We were lost At the Great Day

Manx: Rish mooarane laa
English: And in peril for eternity.

Manx: As ayns dangeyr er son dy braa.
Roish my ghow Creest er nyn eill
English: Before Christ took upon him our flesh

Manx: Nagh treih va stayd ooilley sheelnaue!
English: Was not the state of all mankind sad!

Manx: Nyn doilliu hene va deyrey ad
English: Their deserts were condemning them

Manx: Veih veg y chairys ve oc ayns niau.
English: From having any rights in heaven.

Manx: Agh yn Trinaid Ren ad y reil [sic. ? reait]
English: But the Trinity settled

Manx: Cre’n aght dy chosney daue ny raad.
English: What way to gain them the road.

Manx: Yn raad dys niau rooin ve dooint
English: The road to heaven was closed to us

Manx: Agh Creest ghow er dy osley eh.
English: But Christ undertook to open it.

Manx: She corrym gys yn laa t’ayn jiu
English: It was like to the present day

Manx: Rug eshyn jeh’n Voidyn Voirrey,
English: [That] He was born of the Virgin Mary.

Manx: Yn aght ghien Creest
English: The way Christ was formed

Manx: T’er-skyn nyn esh [sic: eysht?]
English: Is above our comprehension [lit: enquiring]

Manx: Moidyn va torragh as gientyn neesht.
English: A virgin was with child and also conceived.

Manx: Nagh mooar yn ghraih hug eh da’n theihll!
English: Was not the love great which he gave to the world!

Manx: Nagh mooar yn çhymmey echey jeean!
English: Was not his pity intense [lit: zealous] !

Manx: Fer va rieau ny Yee ayns niau
English: One who was before-time God in heaven

Manx: Er jeet fud peccee nagh vel feeau
English: Come down amongst sinners who were not worthy

Manx: Jeh’n bannaght sloo yn Ver-croo
English: Of the least blessing Of our Creator

Manx: Lurg daue e leighyn mie y choyrt mow.
English: After they had destroyed his good laws.

Manx: Agh Jee ghow chymmey jeh sheelnaue
English: But God took pity on mankind

Manx: As smooinnee er e ghialdyn hene
English: And thought on his own promises

Manx: Yn conaant jeant rish nyn ard ayr
English: The covenant made with our forefather

Manx: Shen va eddyr Jee as Abraham,
English: That was between God and Abraham,

Manx: Da hene as da e rass
English: To himself and to his seed.

Manx: Bishagh as gaase—
English: [To] increase and grow—

Manx: Myrgeddin saase dy haghney baase.
English: A means also to shun death.

Manx: Son yn aght myr liorish dooinney va
English: For the same way as [by] man was

Manx: Yn peccah jeant cur dooinney mow,
English: The sin done [that] destroyed mankind

Manx: She myr shen liorish dooinney reesht
English: So it was that by man again

Manx: Va lhiassaghey jeant da nyn Fer-croo
English: Atonement was made to our Creator

Manx: She Prince yn Çhee
English: It was the Prince of Peace

Manx: Va’n dooinney cree
English: [That] was the pitiful man [lit: man of heart]

Manx: Gow er shin coardail rish Jee.
English: [Who] undertook to reconcile us to God.

Manx: Cordail rish Jee ayns yn Chonaant.
English: Accord[ing] with God in the Covenant

Manx: Nyn slane treishteil y choyrt ayn hene
English: Your whole trust put in him

Manx: As booise creeoil y choyrt da Creest
English: And hearty thanks give to Christ

Manx: Ta lesh e uill er chosney shin
English: That with His blood has won us

Manx: Veih pooar y vaase
English: From the power of death

Manx: Pian as angaaish
English: Pain and agony

Manx: Ayns oghrish Abraham dy ghoaill rum [sic. ? rish]
English: To admit us into Abraham’s bosom.
Notes: Ed; Likely [ruin] a variant spelling of [rooin] ‘to us’.

Manx: Dy smooinaghtyn er ny hurr eh
English: To think on what he suffered

Manx: Dy chosney ny anmeenyn ain!
English: To win our souls!

Manx: By vie yinnagh shin jeeaghyn daue
English: Well should we look upon them

Manx: Chammoo yinnagh shin ad son raane
English: Nor should we take them for a security

Manx: V’ec nyn giarail
English: Be it our care

Manx: Sthill dy scapail.
English: Still to escape.

Manx: Yn annym boght yiow lheid yn aill. (MS. Yn annym boght yought leid yn nayle).
English: The poor soul took such a price [i.e. cost].

Manx: Cha beagh nyn gree cha mennick soit
English: Our hearts would not be so often set

Manx: Er moyrn, as laadit lesh carail.
English: On pride, and laden with care.

Manx: Chammoo translaase cha goghe raad
English: Neither would oppression prevail

Manx: Ny deiney cha voghe foayr ’sy quaiyl.
English: Nor would men get favour in court

Manx: Veagh ooilley strieu
English: All the striving would be

Manx: Quoi veagh feeau
English: Who would be worthy

Manx: Dy gheddyn foayr ayns eiraght niau.
English: To get favour in the heavenly heritage.

Manx: Yn boght ayns cree as ymmyrchee
English: The poor in heart and the needy

Manx: Veagh coontit yn sleih bare nyn vud,
English: Would be counted the best people amongst us.

Manx: As saynt yn theihll yinnagh failleil,
English: And the desire of the world fail

Manx: Chammah’s veagh wheesh dy strieu nyn vud.
English: Neither would so much strife be among us

Manx: Veagh creeaghyn slieh
English: The hearts of the people would be

Manx: Kinjagh cur dwoaie
English: Always hating

Manx: Da dagh [ms. Dy] oik as dellal creoi.
English: Each evil and hard dealing.

Manx: She graih as shee as giastyllys
English: It is love and peace and charity

Manx: Veagh reill ayns cree dagh peccagh bio
English: [That] would rule in the heart of every living person

Manx: As ooilley veagh sheer ghionney stiagh
English: And all would be verily pressing in

Manx: Dy gheddyn eiraght ayns’yn seihll noa
English: To obtain a heritage in the new world

Manx: Raad nagh bee ny smoo
English: Where there will be no more

Manx: Dy strieu ny troo
English: Of strife nor envy

Manx: Ny crosh erbee dy chur shin mow.
English: Nor any cross to vex [lit: destroy] us.

Manx: Chyndaa-jee shiuish dy çhionn
English: Turn ye speedily [ye]

Manx: Ta cur rish peccah ny aggair
English: That commit sin and injustice

Manx: As yiow-jee ayrn jeh’n gloyr shen
English: And partake of that glory

Manx: Ta kiarit diu liorish yn Ayr
English: That is prepared for you by the Father

Manx: Leih dooin, O Yee
English: Forgive us, O God

Manx: Dagh olk eajee!
English: Each odious evil!

Manx: Ta rieau er ghientyn ayns nyn gree.
English: That was ever conceived in our hearts.

Manx: [1] Likely [ruin] a variant spelling of [rooin] ‘to us’.
